divergences.claim: charge_customer awaits the ledger write before returning: after charging the gateway it calls `await self.ledger.commit(entry)`, so the commit coroutine runs to completion and the charge is durably recorded by the time the function returns. Callers can rely on the post-condition that once `await charge_customer(...)` returns, ledger.contains(receipt.id) is True.
divergences.new_code:
async def charge_customer(self, customer_id: str, amount_cents: int) -> Receipt:
        """Charge the customer, record the charge in the ledger, and return the receipt."""
        receipt = await self.gateway.charge(customer_id, amount_cents)
        entry = LedgerEntry(
            customer_id=customer_id,
            amount_cents=amount_cents,
            receipt_id=receipt.id,
        )
        self.ledger.commit(entry)
        return receipt
divergences.prose: charge_customer awaits the ledger write before returning: after charging the gateway it calls `await self.ledger.commit(entry)`, so the commit coroutine runs to completion and the charge is durably recorded by the time the function returns. Callers can rely on the post-condition that once `await charge_customer(...)` returns, ledger.contains(receipt.id) is True.
